text.title: Backlog Structure and Grooming Process
text.summary: The five-stage backlog structure (ideas/validated/specified/designed/ready), the four-part weekly grooming process, RICE scoring formula, and MoSCoW scope allocation rules.

rule.statement: Maintain backlog in five stages: ideas/ (raw, not yet validated), validated/ (aligned with vision, feasibility checked), specified/ (has spec.md), designed/ (has design.md), ready/ (has implementation plan and tasks). Only score validated/ items — ideas/ are unscored.
rule.rationale: Scoring unvalidated ideas wastes effort on work that may be dropped. The stage gates ensure agent RICE scoring operates on items with sufficient information to produce meaningful numbers.

section.title: Weekly Grooming Process
section.p: Four parts, run weekly in 30-60 minutes:

list.item: Part 1 (10 min): Review new ideas — assess alignment with vision, move to validated/ or drop.
Part 2 (20 min): Re-score top 20 backlog items with latest data, reorder by priority.
Part 3 (20 min): Refine upcoming items — add detail, clarify ACs, identify dependencies, break into tasks if needed.
Part 4 (10 min): Archive old items, remove duplicates, update estimates, flag blockers.

rule.statement: Never let the backlog exceed 40 unscored items. Schedule agent-assisted triage when it crosses 20 validated items without RICE scores.
rule.rationale: Unscored backlogs default to recency or vocal stakeholder bias, not impact-adjusted effort. An agent can score 20-40 items in one pass given real data inputs.

rule.statement: RICE Score = (Reach × Impact × Confidence) / Effort. Reach: 1-10 (users affected). Impact: 0.25/0.5/1/2/3 (per user). Confidence: 50%/80%/100%. Effort: person-months (0.5/1/2/3/6+). Flag any item lacking real data for Reach or Impact as "estimate-needed".
rule.rationale: Agent-generated RICE numbers are hallucinated if inputs are not grounded in real data. Flagging estimate-needed items prevents false ranking precision.

example.code:
Feature: Add social login (Google, GitHub) Reach: 8 (80% of users would use it) Impact: 1 (medium — convenience feature) Confidence: 80% (competitor data available) Effort: 1 (1 person-month) RICE Score: (8 × 1 × 0.8) / 1 = 6.4
Feature: Add dark mode Reach: 3 (30% of users requested) Impact: 0.5 (cosmetic improvement) Confidence: 100% (clear requirement) Effort: 0.5 (~2 weeks) RICE Score: (3 × 0.5 × 1.0) / 0.5 = 3.0
Decision: Social login (6.4) > Dark mode (3.0). Do social login first.

rule.statement: Must = ~60% of scope, Should = ~20%, Could = ~20%. Document Won't items explicitly with a reason. If Must exceeds 60%, reprioritize — everything being Must means nothing is prioritized.
rule.rationale: All-Must priority is the most common cause of missed milestones. The 60/20/20 split forces prioritization decisions that surface trade-offs before implementation begins.

antipattern.description: Agents marking everything as Must priority.
antipattern.reason: Violates MoSCoW allocation and creates scope overrun risk. Enforce: Must cannot exceed 60% in the prompt explicitly.
